tool.name: ProSeg
tool.description: probabilistic cell segmentation for spatial transcriptomics
tool.command:
## Input preparation ## For CSV: symlink (ProSeg detects gzip from content). ## For parquet: symlink with .parquet extension. ## For tabular: copy and rename to .csv for ProSeg.
#if $input_mode.mode == "table" #if $input_mode.transcripts.ext == "parquet" cp '$input_mode.transcripts' input_data.parquet && #elif $input_mode.transcripts.ext == "tabular" cp '$input_mode.transcripts' input_data.csv && #else ln -s '$input_mode.transcripts' input_data.csv && #end if #end if
## For SpatialData zarr input: unzip the spatialdata.zip archive #if $input_mode.mode == "zarr" python3 -c 'import zipfile,sys;zipfile.ZipFile(sys.argv[1]).extractall("zarr_input_dir")' '$input_mode.zarr_input' && #end if
## ProSeg command proseg
## Input mode flag #if $input_mode.mode == "zarr" --zarr #end if
## Macro tokens, each block defined in macros.xml @PLATFORM_CMD@ @QV_CMD@ @CELLPOSE_CMD@ @SPACERANGER_CMD@ @MORPHOLOGY_CMD@ @CORE_PARAMS_CMD@ @ADVANCED_CMD@ @OPTIONAL_OUTPUTS_CMD@
## Always write the main SpatialData zarr output --output-spatialdata proseg-output.zarr
## Positional input argument #if $input_mode.mode == "table" #if $input_mode.transcripts.ext == "parquet" input_data.parquet #else input_data.csv #end if #else zarr_input_dir #end if
## Error capture 2> proseg_error.log || { cat proseg_error.log; exit 1; }
## Package zarr directory as spatialdata.zip for Galaxy. ## Galaxy creates an empty placeholder at the output path, ## so we write to a temp file then move it into place. && python3 -c 'import zipfile,pathlib,os;zf=zipfile.ZipFile("proseg-output.zip","w",zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED);[zf.write(p,p.relative_to(".")) for p in pathlib.Path("proseg-output.zarr").rglob("*")];zf.close()' && mv proseg-output.zip '$output_spatialdata'
## Move optional outputs to Galaxy output paths #if $outputs.counts && mv counts.parquet '$counts' #end if #if $outputs.expected && mv expected.parquet '$expected' #end if #if $outputs.cellmeta && mv cellmeta.parquet '$cellmeta' #end if #if $outputs.txmeta && mv txmeta.parquet '$txmeta' #end if #if $outputs.gene && mv gene.parquet '$gene' #end if #if $outputs.polygons && gunzip polygons.geojson.gz && mv polygons.geojson '$polygons' #end if #if $outputs.polygon_layers && gunzip polygon_layers.geojson.gz && mv polygon_layers.geojson '$polygon_layers' #end if

param.name: mode
param.label: Input type
param.help: Select the format of your input data.

param.name: transcripts
param.label: Transcript table
param.help: Table of transcript positions with gene identities and preliminary cell assignments. Accepted formats: CSV, Parquet, tabular.

param.name: zarr_input
param.label: SpatialData archive
param.help: SpatialData zarr directory packaged as a spatialdata.zip archive.

tool.help:
ProSeg - Probabilistic Cell Segmentation =========================================
ProSeg segments cells in spatial transcriptomics data by modelling the spatial distribution of transcripts probabilistically using a Cellular Potts Model with MCMC sampling. Unlike image-based segmentation tools, ProSeg uses the transcript positions themselves as the primary signal for boundary determination, making it platform-independent and capable of repositioning transcripts that have diffused away from their cell of origin.
-----
Supported platforms --------------------
- **10x Xenium** -- provide the ``transcripts.csv`` or ``transcripts.parquet`` file - **NanoString CosMx** -- provide the ``sample_tx_file.csv`` (pixel or micron coordinates) - **Vizgen MERSCOPE / MERFISH** -- provide ``detected_transcripts.csv`` - **10x Visium HD** -- requires SpaceRanger barcode mappings parquet file - **Manual** -- map your own column names for any platform
-----
Inputs -------
**Transcript table (required)** A table with one row per detected RNA molecule, containing spatial coordinates (x, y, and optionally z) and a preliminary cell assignment from the instrument segmentation software. This preliminary assignment is used to initialise the number and location of cells. ProSeg refines boundaries but does not introduce new cells that were entirely missed by the prior segmentation. Accepted formats: CSV, Parquet, tabular.
**CellPose masks (optional)** A numpy array (.npy) of pixel-level cell assignments from CellPose or similar tools. If provided, ProSeg uses this for a more detailed initialisation. The mask coordinates must be correctly registered to transcript micron coordinates using the scale and affine transform parameters.
-----
Key parameters ---------------
**Voxel size** X/Y resolution of the spatial grid used during sampling. Smaller values give finer boundaries but require more memory. Typical values: 1.0 um for Xenium, 0.5 um for CosMx.
**Burn-in voxel size** Must be an integer multiple of voxel size (e.g. 2.0 when voxel size is 1.0). A coarser resolution is used during the preliminary burn-in phase to reach a good starting configuration efficiently.
**Sampling iterations** 200 is adequate for testing. Use 500 or more for production results. ProSeg is non-deterministic -- results vary slightly between runs due to the MCMC sampling procedure.
-----
Outputs --------
**SpatialData archive (always)** A spatialdata.zip archive of a zarr directory readable by the Python ``spatialdata`` package. Contains:
- ``sdata.points["transcripts"]`` -- revised transcript positions - ``sdata.shapes["cell_boundaries"]`` -- cell boundary polygons - ``sdata.tables["table"]`` -- AnnData with count matrix in X, cell metadata in obs, gene metadata in var, centroids in obsm["spatial"]
**Count matrix (optional)** Cell-by-gene integer counts in Apache Parquet format. Read with ``pandas.read_parquet``.
**Cell polygons (optional)** 2D cell boundary polygons in GeoJSON format, flattened from 3D.
-----
Known limitations ------------------
- Cells with very few transcripts will have unreliable boundaries. - ProSeg cannot introduce cells missed entirely by the prior segmentation. - In highly homogeneous regions where neighbouring cells express very similar genes, boundary placement is less reliable. - Results vary slightly between runs due to MCMC non-determinism. - If ProSeg crashes without an error message, it likely ran out of memory. Increase voxel size, reduce voxel layers, or enable Disable diffusion model to reduce memory usage.
